figures.caption: Figure 8: Evolved best program by SEAEVOSHINKA in EPLB

Figure 1: Search trajectories on Circle Packing benchmarks. Solid curves show the best-so-far sum of radii over generations, dotted curves show cumulative API cost, and the gray dashed line denotes the Human/SOTA reference. SEAEVO improves both OpenEvolve and ShinkaEvolve backbones, reaching competitive solutions earlier while incurring lower cumulative cost.

Table 2: Performance on systems optimization benchmarks with Gemini-3-Flash. Avg denotes mean score with standard deviation over runs, and Best denotes the best score achieved. The Human/SOTA row reports reference best scores when available. Best method results are shown in bold.

Table 4: Hyperparameter sensitivity on Circle Packing (Square) with Gemini-3-Flash. Baseline is the full SEAEVO configuration (∆=10, k=5, ε=0.2). Each row varies one hyperparameter while holding the others fixed.

Table 3: Ablation study on Circle Packing (Square) with Gemini-3-Flash. We report mean fitness (Avg), standard deviation (Std), best fitness (Best), generation at which best was found (Gen at Best), and average API cost (AvgCost) across three seeds.

Figure 2: Overview of SEAEVO. Three modules enrich the evolutionary loop: Strategy Articulation replaces blind mutation with a diagnose-direct-implement pipeline; Stratified Experience Retrieval assembles context from semantically clustered, behaviorally diverse archive entries; Strategic Landscape Navigation periodically diagnoses which strategy families are effective, saturated, or underexplored, and steers future mutations accordingly.
Figure 5: Strategy evolution timeline for EPLB with SEAEVOSHINKA
Figure 6: Strategy evolution timeline for Prism with SEAEVOSHINKA
Figure 7: Evolved best program by SEAEVOSHINKA in Prism

Figure 4: Performance on the XSTest agent scaffold benchmark. Bars show average test score with standard deviation across runs, and diamonds indicate the best score. We report results under single-call and three-call inference settings.

Figure 3: Strategy embedding spaces on two system optimization benchmarks. Each point denotes a candidate program projected from its strategy embedding by t-SNE, color indicates the combined score, and marker shape distinguishes the base backbone from SEAEVO-augmented search.
Table 6: Full-precision values for results that appear tied after rounding in Table 1, including Circle Packing (Rectangle, n=21) and MinMax Distance.
